performance_report.environment: Next.js 16.2.10 production build (npm run build), measured via Turbopack

contributing_factors: Static page prerendering — HTML served from edge CDN
No external image resources — all SVG is inline
No third-party JS libraries (no React Query, no Redux, no Tailwind runtime)
BroadcastChannel events handled in O(1) with simple spread merge
No virtualized lists or complex re-renders on user interaction
Button handlers are async but non-blocking (fire-and-forget pattern for state broadcast)
next/font with display:swap prevents layout shift from font loading
All SVG viewBox is fixed — no dimensions from external resources
No dynamic ad slots or async content insertions above the fold

bundle_analysis.no_ui_library: No Tailwind CSS, no Material UI, no Radix UI — pure vanilla CSS reduces parse cost
bundle_analysis.no_charting_lib: All data visualization uses inline SVG — zero charting library overhead

security_headers_performance.note: HTTP security headers add negligible overhead (< 1ms) and prevent entire categories of attacks (XSS, clickjacking) that would otherwise require runtime JS protections

rendering_strategy.model: Static Site Generation (SSG) for all user-facing pages
rendering_strategy.rationale: All initial page state is deterministic (idle simulation). Dynamic state arrives via BroadcastChannel/Firestore after hydration. This maximizes CDN hit rate and minimizes TTFB.
rendering_strategy.api_routes: Server-rendered on demand — isolates API key environment from static bundle

optimization_applied: next/font: eliminates render-blocking Google Fonts request
Inline SVG: eliminates external image HTTP requests
No barrel imports: prevents unintended module tree-shaking failures
prefers-reduced-motion: prevents costly CSS animations for users with vestibular disorders
Static prerender: all 4 views served as pre-built HTML from CDN edge
